docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9152:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6500:If a clansman receives bandit-drop loot above carrying capacity:
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9220:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7697:`ClanState.DEAD` is the right anchor for Phase 7.5 OTC restrictions, and status codes were appended rather than inserted, which preserves existing numeric values. The death helper is close, but before merge it needs to become the single reliable lifecycle path for starvation/cold/bandit death, including vault burn, mission cleanup, defender release, and consistent events.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9221:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7736:`ClanState.DEAD` is the right anchor for Phase 7.5 OTC restrictions, and status codes were appended rather than inserted, which preserves existing numeric values. The death helper is close, but before merge it needs to become the single reliable lifecycle path for starvation/cold/bandit death, including vault burn, mission cleanup, defender release, and consistent events.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9226:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:79:Per spec §8.3 ("no minOut in v1") this is **spec-compliant**. Worth flagging because it's an asymmetry with BUY's `maxGoldIn` and a soft attack surface for adversarial elders that try to sandwich same-tick scheduled sells with their own immediate trades (spec §11.2 explicitly admits immediate-may-front-run-scheduled). Document that this is intentional v1 behavior in the elder toolbelt docs so agents don't expect price guarantees on sells.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9227:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-synthesis.md:11:The market plumbing is structurally sound (immediate + scheduled FIFO, k-invariant AMM, heartbeat ordering preserved, 116/116 tests). All 3 reviewers independently flag the same fundamental finding: **market trades operate on the clan vault, not worker carry — violating spec §8.3-8.4.** The impl + tests are mutually consistent but the spec contract is broken. Implications cascade into bandit loot timing + physical-presence game design.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9228:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-synthesis.md:16:- Path A (spec-correct): rewrite trades to operate on worker carry. Invasive — touches 4 functions + ~15 tests + bandit interaction. ~1-2 hour fix-round.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9229:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-synthesis.md:55:4. Verify bandit-loot timing tests still pass (vault changes vs carry changes)
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9231:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:28:5. Integration risks — interaction with Phase 5 vault state + Phase 9 bandit attack timing

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9278:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10297:ERC20 balances are **not** the authoritative representation of a clan’s vault or a clansman’s wheelbarrow.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9281:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10903:NEEDS_FIXES. The heartbeat/FIFO plumbing is close, but two core Phase 6 invariants are off: market trades are wired to clan vault balances instead of worker carry, and the new ERC20 boundary layer is only seeded, not actually bridged during swaps. There’s also spec drift in when scheduled actions enter the queue and how immediate actions surface mission state, which will leak into UI/indexer behavior and later bandit/upkeep interactions.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9282:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10907:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9284:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10925:- The biggest integrated consequence is cross-phase: routing market buys/sells through vault balances instead of worker carry materially changes Phase 5 deposit logistics and Phase 9 bandit timing.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9285:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10935:NEEDS_FIXES. The heartbeat/FIFO plumbing is close, but two core Phase 6 invariants are off: market trades are wired to clan vault balances instead of worker carry, and the new ERC20 boundary layer is only seeded, not actually bridged during swaps. There’s also spec drift in when scheduled actions enter the queue and how immediate actions surface mission state, which will leak into UI/indexer behavior and later bandit/upkeep interactions.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9286:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10939:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10346:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5455:docs/reviews/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1839:+            perGold = _perClanBanditLootShare(bandit.carryGold, nDefendingClans);
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10671:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3708:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:494:The `BanditAttackResolved` event emits hardcoded `0`s for the `stolenWood`, `stolenIron`, `stolenWheat`, and `stolenFish` fields even if the bandit escapes successfully. If resource stealing is deferred to a future phase, it should be documented as a stub; otherwise, the bandit should actually drain resources from the target clan's vault upon an un-defeated attack.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10823:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4283:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11590:/bin/bash -lc "rg -n \"BanditState\\.(NONE|CAMPING|RESTING|ATTACKING|DEFEATED|ESCAPED|None|Spawned|Camped|Resting|Attacking|Defeated|Escaped)|banditId|currentRegion|attackAttemptsMade|stateEnteredTick|nextActionTick|tier|attackPower|carryGold|getBandit\\(|getBanditTroop\\(|getBanditsInRegion\\(|LootDistributed\\(|BlueprintEarned|BanditTargetDied|WallDamagedByBandit|ClansmanKilledByBandit\" . --glob '"'!**/node_modules/**'"'" in /home/claude/code/clan-world
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10935:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4455:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14321:23f7f1a:packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:56:        _bandits[banditId].carryGold = gold;
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10972:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5297:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:694:This tiebreak is deterministic and prevents bandit targeting from stalling or becoming ambiguous when multiple bases have identical vault values.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10975:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5384:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:933:- only vault resources count for upkeep, winter burn, building, and bandit target value
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10980:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5466:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:52:This requirement exists because bandit targeting depends on each base's current vault loot-value, and accurate loot-value requires up-to-date clan settlement.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10981:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5648:docs/reviews/pr198-synthesis.md:11:The market plumbing is structurally sound (immediate + scheduled FIFO, k-invariant AMM, heartbeat ordering preserved, 116/116 tests). All 3 reviewers independently flag the same fundamental finding: **market trades operate on the clan vault, not worker carry — violating spec §8.3-8.4.** The impl + tests are mutually consistent but the spec contract is broken. Implications cascade into bandit loot timing + physical-presence game design.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10984:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5685:docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:404:score = vaultGold + (workers * N) + (wallTier * M) + (monumentTier * K) - banditDamageTaken
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11006:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6063:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9423:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:933:- only vault resources count for upkeep, winter burn, building, and bandit target value
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11007:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6081:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10907:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11008:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6082:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10939:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11013:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6250:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10325:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11014:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6252:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10360:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11016:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6259:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:79:Per spec §8.3 ("no minOut in v1") this is **spec-compliant**. Worth flagging because it's an asymmetry with BUY's `maxGoldIn` and a soft attack surface for adversarial elders that try to sandwich same-tick scheduled sells with their own immediate trades (spec §11.2 explicitly admits immediate-may-front-run-scheduled). Document that this is intentional v1 behavior in the elder toolbelt docs so agents don't expect price guarantees on sells.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11041:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6552:docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:195:| Resources become useful | World: wheelbarrow fill animation, deposit animation at base. Inspector: vault counter ticks up |
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11060:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6930:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:31:**Impact:** A bandit could spawn into a region where bases and defenders were NOT eager-settled, meaning the very next attack resolution sees stale clan state. This is a correctness bug, not a security bug — the clan will settle lazily later, but attack damage calculations against stale vaults/defender counts could be wrong.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11065:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7235:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:52:This requirement exists because bandit targeting depends on each base's current vault loot-value, and accurate loot-value requires up-to-date clan settlement.

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:12824:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14972:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1548, 3473-3503` and `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:455-473` — Several legacy bandit-facing fields are still exposed but now carry placeholder zeroes (`tier`, `attackAttemptsMade`, `maxAttemptsRemaining`, `projectedTargetLootValue`). That silently degrades downstream behavior instead of failing loudly. Suggested fix: either compute real values or version/remove the legacy fields and update consumers.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:12830:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:15014:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1548, 3473-3503` and `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:455-473` — Several legacy bandit-facing fields are still exposed but now carry placeholder zeroes (`tier`, `attackAttemptsMade`, `maxAttemptsRemaining`, `projectedTargetLootValue`). That silently degrades downstream behavior instead of failing loudly. Suggested fix: either compute real values or version/remove the legacy fields and update consumers.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:12841:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:494:The `BanditAttackResolved` event emits hardcoded `0`s for the `stolenWood`, `stolenIron`, `stolenWheat`, and `stolenFish` fields even if the bandit escapes successfully. If resource stealing is deferred to a future phase, it should be documented as a stub; otherwise, the bandit should actually drain resources from the target clan's vault upon an un-defeated attack.

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13468:  2505	        // Place worker at UT WAITING with carry wood
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13472:  2509	        uint256 carryBefore = world.getClansman(csId).carryWood;
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13473:  2510	        uint256 vaultBefore = world.getClan(clanId).vaultWood;
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13481:  2518	            action: ActionType.MarketSell,
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13491:  2528	        assertEq(world.getClansman(csId).carryWood, carryBefore - 5e18, "sell: carry must decrease by sell amount");
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13492:  2529	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).vaultWood, vaultBefore, "sell: vault must be unchanged");
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13497:  2534	        // Setup: worker in UT with empty carry but vault has wood
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13501:  2538	        // Advance cs to UT with no carry (no gather)
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13506:  2543	        // Clan starts with vault wood from minting; carry is 0
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13507:  2544	        assertEq(world.getClansman(csId).carryWood, 0, "carry must be 0");
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13508:  2545	        assertGt(world.getClan(clanId).vaultWood, 0, "vault must have wood");
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13515:  2552	            action: ActionType.MarketSell,
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13523:  2560	        // Immediate path: carry empty → ERR_MISSING_RESOURCES or market failure
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13525:  2562	        // With carry-based validation, immediate sell should fail
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13526:  2563	        assertNotEq(uint8(results[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "empty carry sell must not succeed");
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13531:I’ve found one likely real regression already: the new “empty carry sell” test and the current market code disagree on behavior, which usually means either the fix is incomplete or the new test was written against a rule the contract still doesn’t enforce. I’m checking the surrounding API consumers now so we can separate contract bugs from test-only gaps.

docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:39:`ClanWorld.sol:~3473-3510` populates `carryWood/Iron/Wheat/Fish/Gold` and `attackPower` from the new `BanditTroop`, but `attackAttemptsMade`, `maxAttemptsRemaining`, and `tier` are still hard-zeroed. If the UI uses these to gate "danger" indicators, they'll always read 0. Either remove from the view struct or wire them up.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:63:2. **ABI redesign is clean.** `BanditTroop` and `BanditState` got a clean rewrite (`internalType: "enum BanditState"` reorder, `tickEnteredState`/`region`/`strength`/`carryGold` rename and additions). The interface comment explicitly flags ABI consumers must regenerate. Both the engine and the stub are aligned. New `getBandit` / `getBanditsInRegion` view methods plus the `getBanditTroop` shim look complete.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_3_schema_patch.md:202:- reads currently committed vault balances only
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_3_schema_patch.md:275:`ERR_CARRY_FULL` is especially relevant for explicit market buy validation and any direct action that is rejected because requested output cannot fit remaining carry capacity.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:19:Phase 8 ships: wall upgrades (8.1) + base upgrades (8.2) + monument upgrades w/ blueprint gating L7-10 (8.3) + score / rank getters (8.4). Builds on Phase 5 vault + Phase 9 blueprint reward.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:26:5. Phase 8.4 leaderboard correctness (uses derived getters per #261 fix)

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5041:docs/reviews/pr153-review-claude-opus.md:34:| 2 | `runner/src/runnerCastHeartbeat.ts` | 51–56 | **Runner heartbeat targets World Chain Sepolia (chainId 4801) instead of Base Sepolia (84532).** After the Base Sepolia chain pivot (PR #125), `RealChainClient`, `foundry.toml`, `start-heartbeat-loop.sh`, and `deployments/base-sepolia.json` all target chainId 84532. But `RunnerCastHeartbeat` still defines `worldChainSepolia` with `id: 4801`. Signed transactions carry wrong chain ID — they will fail, target the wrong network, or suffer replay-protection mismatch. Wave 2 confirmed this is stale config, not intentional. | Correctness |
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5044:docs/reviews/pr153-review-claude-opus.md:137:Agent 1.6 flagged a chain config split. Agent 2.1 and 2.3 both independently confirmed: `runnerCastHeartbeat.ts` defines `worldChainSepolia` with `id: 4801`, while every other component (IChainClient, foundry.toml, deployments/base-sepolia.json, start-heartbeat-loop.sh) targets Base Sepolia (`id: 84532`). This is stale configuration from before the Base Sepolia pivot (PR #125). Signed transactions carry wrong chain ID and will fail or target the wrong network.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5265:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:100:Homebases may exist across the world but may **not** be placed in Unicorn Town.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5266:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:110:Deep Sea and Unicorn Town are not valid homebase spawn regions.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5282:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:398:Each homebase has an effectively infinite vault.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5285:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:412:A clansman must be at their own homebase and perform `deposit_resources` for carried resources to enter the clan vault.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5292:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:676:Bandit target selection is based on **current vault state at attack resolution time**, not at spawn time.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5297:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:694:This tiebreak is deterministic and prevents bandit targeting from stalling or becoming ambiguous when multiple bases have identical vault values.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5313:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:809:The defended base vault receives:
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5324:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:885:All building actions consume resources from the homebase vault, not from carried worker inventory.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5352:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:1164:- only vault resources count for upkeep and building
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5373:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:227:Carried resources only enter the clan vault after `DepositResources` resolves at homebase.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5383:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:818:Because upkeep is applied before deposit effects, a worker arriving home with wood/food in carry does **not** save the base from that same tick’s winter burn or upkeep shortage.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5384:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:933:- only vault resources count for upkeep, winter burn, building, and bandit target value

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5625:docs/reviews/pr200-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6448:packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:1759:        // Send clansman to homebase to DepositResources (empty carry -> completes when the arrival tick closes).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5646:docs/reviews/pr200-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7104:  Proposals store `from`, `to`, amounts, expiry, and flags, but not the proposer address, owner at proposal time, or an owner/IFT nonce. `accept*Transfer` validates only the current target owner. If `fromClan.owner` changes after proposal creation, the target can still accept and debit the new owner’s clan. This affects gold, vault, blueprint, and bundled transfers. Store proposer/owner nonce and require it at accept, or invalidate open proposals on ownership transfer.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5647:docs/reviews/pr200-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7155:  Proposals store `from`, `to`, amounts, expiry, and flags, but not the proposer address, owner at proposal time, or an owner/IFT nonce. `accept*Transfer` validates only the current target owner. If `fromClan.owner` changes after proposal creation, the target can still accept and debit the new owner’s clan. This affects gold, vault, blueprint, and bundled transfers. Store proposer/owner nonce and require it at accept, or invalidate open proposals on ownership transfer.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5648:docs/reviews/pr198-synthesis.md:11:The market plumbing is structurally sound (immediate + scheduled FIFO, k-invariant AMM, heartbeat ordering preserved, 116/116 tests). All 3 reviewers independently flag the same fundamental finding: **market trades operate on the clan vault, not worker carry — violating spec §8.3-8.4.** The impl + tests are mutually consistent but the spec contract is broken. Implications cascade into bandit loot timing + physical-presence game design.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5649:docs/reviews/pr198-synthesis.md:17:- Path B (impl-correct): patch spec §8.3-8.4 to match vault-based trades. Document deviation. Zero code change. Spec amendment.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5650:docs/reviews/pr198-synthesis.md:23:| H1: `ClanWorld.sol:1657, 1669, 1825, 1773, 1885+` | 5.4 + 5.5 + 4.7 = **3/3** | HIGH | **Market sell/buy bypass worker carry, mutate vault directly.** Spec §8.3: sell = worker carry → gold purse. §8.4: buy = gold purse → worker carry. Impl: sells `_deductFromVault`, buys `_addToVault`. BUY internally inconsistent — validates `_remainingCarryForToken` (correct gate) then writes to vault. Tests codify the broken behavior. Fixing requires test updates too. **Game-design impact:** worker physically in Unicorn Town is irrelevant — only homebase vault is consulted. Bandit loot timing changes (vault drains immediately vs eventually). Bought goods are instantly upkeep-eligible without a return trip. |
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5667:docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:336:**Setup:** Clan starts at `wallTier = 0`, `wallHpCurrent = 0`. Clan has required stone in vault.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5670:docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:348:**Setup:** Clan has `wallTier = 2`, `wallHpCurrent = 50` (damaged). Sufficient stone in vault.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5685:docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:404:score = vaultGold + (workers * N) + (wallTier * M) + (monumentTier * K) - banditDamageTaken
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5687:docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:411:**Setup:** Clan A has higher score than Clan B. Bandit attacks Clan A (defender loss) — Clan A takes vault loss.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5688:docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:415:**Assert:** Scores reflect vault/wall damage. Rank may have changed if Clan B now scores higher.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6062:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9421:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:818:Because upkeep is applied before deposit effects, a worker arriving home with wood/food in carry does **not** save the base from that same tick’s winter burn or upkeep shortage.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6063:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9423:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:933:- only vault resources count for upkeep, winter burn, building, and bandit target value
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6081:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10907:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6082:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10939:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6203:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5927:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:100:Homebases may exist across the world but may **not** be placed in Unicorn Town.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6204:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5928:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:110:Deep Sea and Unicorn Town are not valid homebase spawn regions.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6250:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10325:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6251:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10335:- `packages/contracts/test/HeartbeatOrdering.t.sol` encodes the vault-based market model: one clansman deposits into vault and another sells from vault in the same heartbeat. That protects heartbeat ordering, but it masks the carry-accounting regression above.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6252:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10360:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6253:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10370:- `packages/contracts/test/HeartbeatOrdering.t.sol` encodes the vault-based market model: one clansman deposits into vault and another sells from vault in the same heartbeat. That protects heartbeat ordering, but it masks the carry-accounting regression above.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6254:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:7:**Verdict: NEEDS_FIXES.** Phase 6 ships a working immediate + scheduled market with FIFO ordering, k-invariant-checked AMM, deterministic heartbeat ordering, and 116/116 green tests. But the resource-side accounting **inverts the spec**: §8.3-8.4 require SELL to source from worker carry and BUY to credit worker carry, while the implementation drains/credits the clan vault on both sides — and the BUY path is even internally contradictory (validates `_remainingCarryForToken` then writes to `_addToVault`). Two other meaningful concerns: the per-tick scheduled-queue cap was deleted in the fix-round leaving an O(n²) storage-sort with no upper bound, and the `MinimalERC20.mint/burn` "boundary" is dead code never invoked by the engine.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6255:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:11:### H1 — Market action source/destination violates spec §8.3 + §8.4 (carry vs vault)
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6256:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:13:Spec text (`docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md` §8.3-8.4):
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6257:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:24:The buy path is **internally contradictory** — it gates on worker carry capacity but mutates vault, so the carry-cap check no longer reflects what the action actually does. Practical consequences: a worker with full carry but an empty vault is rejected from buying; a worker physically present at UT with carry to sell is irrelevant — only the clan's homebase vault is consulted (vault is at the homebase, far from UT).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6259:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:79:Per spec §8.3 ("no minOut in v1") this is **spec-compliant**. Worth flagging because it's an asymmetry with BUY's `maxGoldIn` and a soft attack surface for adversarial elders that try to sandwich same-tick scheduled sells with their own immediate trades (spec §11.2 explicitly admits immediate-may-front-run-scheduled). Document that this is intentional v1 behavior in the elder toolbelt docs so agents don't expect price guarantees on sells.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6263:docs/reviews/pr137-review-claude-opus.md:66:| 15 | `src/ClanWorld.sol` | 1078–1103 | **Leading underscore on `external` functions.** `_executeMarketSellExternal` / `_executeMarketBuyExternal` use internal naming convention on external visibility. Justified by try/catch pattern but unconventional. Agent 7. | **DEFER** |

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6915:docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:364:    description: 'Transfer gold, blueprints, or vault resources to another clan. No protocol-enforced reciprocity; trust is social.',
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6930:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:31:**Impact:** A bandit could spawn into a region where bases and defenders were NOT eager-settled, meaning the very next attack resolution sees stale clan state. This is a correctness bug, not a security bug — the clan will settle lazily later, but attack damage calculations against stale vaults/defender counts could be wrong.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6967:docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_master_coordination.md:153:Agent runner observation rendering (spec §8.3) consumes `recent` as chronologically ordered (oldest → newest, so trend direction is "left to right increasing").

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7307:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:412:A clansman must be at their own homebase and perform `deposit_resources` for carried resources to enter the clan vault.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7314:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:676:Bandit target selection is based on **current vault state at attack resolution time**, not at spawn time.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7319:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:694:This tiebreak is deterministic and prevents bandit targeting from stalling or becoming ambiguous when multiple bases have identical vault values.

This contradicts `IClanWorld.sol`, `clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md §8.3–8.4`, and `clanworld_v4_3_schema_patch.md`.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7392:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:227:Carried resources only enter the clan vault after `DepositResources` resolves at homebase.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7402:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:818:Because upkeep is applied before deposit effects, a worker arriving home with wood/food in carry does **not** save the base from that same tick’s winter burn or upkeep shortage.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7403:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:933:- only vault resources count for upkeep, winter burn, building, and bandit target value

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12489:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4715:packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2284:    ///      for the same level and tick, committed vault loot value wins; wall level is the final
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12516:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5648:docs/reviews/pr198-synthesis.md:11:The market plumbing is structurally sound (immediate + scheduled FIFO, k-invariant AMM, heartbeat ordering preserved, 116/116 tests). All 3 reviewers independently flag the same fundamental finding: **market trades operate on the clan vault, not worker carry — violating spec §8.3-8.4.** The impl + tests are mutually consistent but the spec contract is broken. Implications cascade into bandit loot timing + physical-presence game design.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12520:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6250:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10325:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12521:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6252:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10360:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:13998:packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1267:        // Starting vault per v4 spec §12.4
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14037:`quoteLootValueSettled()` still returns raw `_lootValueRaw(_clans[clanId])`, and `_getClanScore()` reads `_clans[clanId]` directly. That violates the Phase 8.4 / #261 expectation that leaderboard scoring uses settled/derived getters. A clan with unsettled upkeep, deposits, deaths, or vault changes can be mis-ranked until someone mutates settlement.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14105:`quoteLootValueSettled()` still returns raw `_lootValueRaw(_clans[clanId])`, and `_getClanScore()` reads `_clans[clanId]` directly. That violates the Phase 8.4 / #261 expectation that leaderboard scoring uses settled/derived getters. A clan with unsettled upkeep, deposits, deaths, or vault changes can be mis-ranked until someone mutates settlement.

docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14373:23f7f1a:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:809:The defended base vault receives:
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14424:   213	- Defender clan vault unchanged (no vault damage).
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14439:   228	- Defender clan vault decremented by bandit carry amount.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14490:   676	Bandit target selection is based on **current vault state at attack resolution time**, not at spawn time.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14496:   682	- moving resources out of vault into worker inventories
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14508:   694	This tiebreak is deterministic and prevents bandit targeting from stalling or becoming ambiguous when multiple bases have identical vault values.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14511:   697	Bandits target by fixed weighted vault value:
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14518:   704	Only vault physical resources are counted.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14527:   713	Bandits never enter Unicorn Town or Deep Sea.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14603:   780	- bandits steal `20%` of each vault physical resource:
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14615:   792	Bandits carry stolen physical resources internally until:
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14620:   797	Bandits do not use Unicorn Town in v1.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14628:   805	- any clansman loot above carrying capacity is burned
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14632:   809	The defended base vault receives:

docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14972:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1548, 3473-3503` and `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:455-473` — Several legacy bandit-facing fields are still exposed but now carry placeholder zeroes (`tier`, `attackAttemptsMade`, `maxAttemptsRemaining`, `projectedTargetLootValue`). That silently degrades downstream behavior instead of failing loudly. Suggested fix: either compute real values or version/remove the legacy fields and update consumers.

docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:15014:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1548, 3473-3503` and `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:455-473` — Several legacy bandit-facing fields are still exposed but now carry placeholder zeroes (`tier`, `attackAttemptsMade`, `maxAttemptsRemaining`, `projectedTargetLootValue`). That silently degrades downstream behavior instead of failing loudly. Suggested fix: either compute real values or version/remove the legacy fields and update consumers.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:39:3 = Unicorn Town
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:100:Homebases may exist across the world but may **not** be placed in Unicorn Town.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:110:Deep Sea and Unicorn Town are not valid homebase spawn regions.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:212:- goto Unicorn Town, sell wood
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:215:- goto noop, sell wood immediately in Unicorn Town
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:273:- arrives to build but vault lacks required materials
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:323:V1 only includes **immediate market actions in Unicorn Town**.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:340:This is especially important for immediate market actions in Unicorn Town.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:384:### Per-clansman carrying caps
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:398:Each homebase has an effectively infinite vault.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:412:A clansman must be at their own homebase and perform `deposit_resources` for carried resources to enter the clan vault.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:434:If a gathering tick would exceed remaining carrying capacity for a resource, the yield is **clamped** to remaining capacity.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:467:Each tick, each clan consumes from vault:
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:474:If a clan vault cannot satisfy required wheat or fish upkeep during a tick:
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:495:If a clansman receives bandit-drop loot above carrying capacity:
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:502:# 5. Unicorn Town Market Model
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:505:Unicorn Town is the only region where Uniswap market actions may be executed in v1.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:525:- worker must travel to Unicorn Town first
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:530:- `goto Unicorn Town, action = sell wood`
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:534:- one action tick in Unicorn Town is consumed
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:536:- worker becomes `WAITING` in Unicorn Town
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:540:- clansman is physically in Unicorn Town
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:555:- the worker remains in Unicorn Town
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:567:This intentionally allows a clansman already stationed in Unicorn Town to front-run scheduled trades that will execute at tick close.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:585:- immediate market actions executed in Elder txs while already in Unicorn Town
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:597:- clansman becomes or remains `WAITING` in Unicorn Town
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:638:Bandits may not spawn in Unicorn Town or Deep Sea.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:676:Bandit target selection is based on **current vault state at attack resolution time**, not at spawn time.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:682:- moving resources out of vault into worker inventories
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:694:This tiebreak is deterministic and prevents bandit targeting from stalling or becoming ambiguous when multiple bases have identical vault values.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:697:Bandits target by fixed weighted vault value:
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:704:Only vault physical resources are counted.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:713:Bandits never enter Unicorn Town or Deep Sea.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:780:- bandits steal `20%` of each vault physical resource:
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:792:Bandits carry stolen physical resources internally until:
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:797:Bandits do not use Unicorn Town in v1.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:805:- any clansman loot above carrying capacity is burned
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:809:The defended base vault receives:
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:870:Wall damage and clansman deaths caused by earlier winter cold still persist, but the temporary cold-damage accumulator does not carry into the next winter cycle.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:885:All building actions consume resources from the homebase vault, not from carried worker inventory.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:887:## 8.3 Wall levels
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:901:## 8.4 Base levels
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:942:- if required resources are present in vault, the action succeeds and consumes one action tick
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:959:3. highest surviving vault loot-value
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:1095:## 12.4 Starting vault contents
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:1108:This is intended to enable at least one meaningful early Unicorn Town trade.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:1127:- all remaining vault resources are **burned**
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:1164:- only vault resources count for upkeep and building
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:1165:- bandits cannot spawn in Unicorn Town or Deep Sea
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:1168:- immediate market actions require the worker to be in Unicorn Town and `WAITING`
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:1187:- vault-only survival and construction counting
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:1201:- dead clans are eliminated and remaining vault resources are burned
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:1208:- starter vault and gold amounts

docs/planning/CANONICAL_SPEC.md:29:**The conflict:** `clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md §A7` originally stated "All v1 market actions are Exact Input actions only" and described `market_buy` as spending an exact amount of gold. This contradicts `IClanWorld.sol`, `clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md §8.3–8.4`, and `clanworld_v4_3_schema_patch.md`.

docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:100:Homebases may exist across the world but may **not** be placed in Unicorn Town.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:110:Deep Sea and Unicorn Town are not valid homebase spawn regions.

docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:323:V1 only includes **immediate market actions in Unicorn Town**.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:340:This is especially important for immediate market actions in Unicorn Town.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:384:### Per-clansman carrying caps
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:398:Each homebase has an effectively infinite vault.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:412:A clansman must be at their own homebase and perform `deposit_resources` for carried resources to enter the clan vault.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:434:If a gathering tick would exceed remaining carrying capacity for a resource, the yield is **clamped** to remaining capacity.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:467:Each tick, each clan consumes from vault:
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:474:If a clan vault cannot satisfy required wheat or fish upkeep during a tick:
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:495:If a clansman receives bandit-drop loot above carrying capacity:
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:502:# 5. Unicorn Town Market Model
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:505:Unicorn Town is the only region where Uniswap market actions may be executed in v1.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:525:- worker must travel to Unicorn Town first
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:530:- `goto Unicorn Town, action = sell wood`
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:534:- one action tick in Unicorn Town is consumed
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:536:- worker becomes `WAITING` in Unicorn Town
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:540:- clansman is physically in Unicorn Town
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:555:- the worker remains in Unicorn Town
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:567:This intentionally allows a clansman already stationed in Unicorn Town to front-run scheduled trades that will execute at tick close.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:585:- immediate market actions executed in Elder txs while already in Unicorn Town
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:597:- clansman becomes or remains `WAITING` in Unicorn Town
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:638:Bandits may not spawn in Unicorn Town or Deep Sea.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:676:Bandit target selection is based on **current vault state at attack resolution time**, not at spawn time.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:682:- moving resources out of vault into worker inventories
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:694:This tiebreak is deterministic and prevents bandit targeting from stalling or becoming ambiguous when multiple bases have identical vault values.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:697:Bandits target by fixed weighted vault value:
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:704:Only vault physical resources are counted.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:713:Bandits never enter Unicorn Town or Deep Sea.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:780:- bandits steal `20%` of each vault physical resource:
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:792:Bandits carry stolen physical resources internally until:
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:797:Bandits do not use Unicorn Town in v1.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:805:- any clansman loot above carrying capacity is burned
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:809:The defended base vault receives:
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:870:Wall damage and clansman deaths caused by earlier winter cold still persist, but the temporary cold-damage accumulator does not carry into the next winter cycle.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:885:All building actions consume resources from the homebase vault, not from carried worker inventory.

  2496	    function test_marketSell_deductsFromCarry_notVault() public {
  2497	        // Use harness to directly set carry and test immediate sell
  2498	        ClanWorldTestHarness harness = new ClanWorldTestHarness();
  2499	        world = harness;
  2500	        _setupMarket();
  2501	        uint32 clanId = _mintClan();
  2502	        ClanFullView memory v = world.getClanFullView(clanId);
  2503	        uint32 csId = v.clansmen[0].clansman.clansman.clansmanId;
  2504	
  2505	        // Place worker at UT WAITING with carry wood
  2506	        harness.setClansmanForTest(csId, ClansmanState.WAITING, ClanWorldConstants.REGION_UNICORN_TOWN, 0);
  2507	        harness.setCarry(csId, 10e18, 0, 0, 0);
  2508	
  2509	        uint256 carryBefore = world.getClansman(csId).carryWood;
  2510	        uint256 vaultBefore = world.getClan(clanId).vaultWood;
  2511	        uint256 goldBefore = world.getClan(clanId).goldBalance;
  2512	
  2513	        address woodTok = world.getTreasuryState().woodToken;
  2514	        ClanOrder[] memory orders = new ClanOrder[](1);
  2515	        orders[0] = ClanOrder({
  2516	            clansmanId: csId,
  2517	            gotoRegion: ClanWorldConstants.REGION_UNICORN_TOWN,
  2518	            action: ActionType.MarketSell,
  2519	            targetClanId: 0,
  2520	            marketToken: woodTok,
  2521	            marketAmount: 5e18,
  2522	            maxGoldIn: 0
  2523	        });
  2524	        vm.prank(elder);
  2525	        OrderResult[] memory results = world.submitClanOrders(clanId, orders);
  2526	
  2527	        assertEq(uint8(results[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "immediate sell must succeed");
  2528	        assertEq(world.getClansman(csId).carryWood, carryBefore - 5e18, "sell: carry must decrease by sell amount");
  2529	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).vaultWood, vaultBefore, "sell: vault must be unchanged");
  2530	        assertGt(world.getClan(clanId).goldBalance, goldBefore, "sell: gold must increase");
  2531	    }
  2532	
  2533	    function test_marketSell_fails_emptyCarry_fullVault() public {
  2534	        // Setup: worker in UT with empty carry but vault has wood
  2535	        uint32 clanId = _mintClan();
  2536	        ClanFullView memory v = world.getClanFullView(clanId);
  2537	        uint32 csId = v.clansmen[0].clansman.clansman.clansmanId;
  2538	        // Advance cs to UT with no carry (no gather)
  2539	        _submitOrder(clanId, csId, ClanWorldConstants.REGION_UNICORN_TOWN, ActionType.Wait);
  2540	        (uint8 travelToUT,) = world.quoteTravel(v.clan.clan.baseRegion, ClanWorldConstants.REGION_UNICORN_TOWN);
  2541	        for (uint256 i = 0; i < uint256(travelToUT) + 2; i++) _advanceTick();
  2542	        world.settleClansman(csId);
  2543	        // Clan starts with vault wood from minting; carry is 0
  2544	        assertEq(world.getClansman(csId).carryWood, 0, "carry must be 0");
  2545	        assertGt(world.getClan(clanId).vaultWood, 0, "vault must have wood");
  2546	
  2547	        address woodTok = world.getTreasuryState().woodToken;
  2548	        ClanOrder[] memory orders = new ClanOrder[](1);
  2549	        orders[0] = ClanOrder({
  2550	            clansmanId: csId,
  2551	            gotoRegion: ClanWorldConstants.REGION_UNICORN_TOWN,
  2552	            action: ActionType.MarketSell,
  2553	            targetClanId: 0,
  2554	            marketToken: woodTok,
  2555	            marketAmount: 1e18,
  2556	            maxGoldIn: 0
  2557	        });
  2558	        vm.prank(elder);
  2559	        OrderResult[] memory results = world.submitClanOrders(clanId, orders);
  2560	        // Immediate path: carry empty → ERR_MISSING_RESOURCES or market failure
  2561	        // Either the order returns ERR_MISSING_RESOURCES at submit, or it's enqueued and fails at execution
  2562	        // With carry-based validation, immediate sell should fail
  2563	        assertNotEq(uint8(results[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "empty carry sell must not succeed");
  2564	    }

   775	    function test_immediateMarketSell_executesInSubmitTx() public {
   776	        (ClanWorldTestHarness harness, address woodAddr, uint32 clanId, uint32 csId) =
   777	            _setupHarnessClanAt(ClansmanState.WAITING, ClanWorldConstants.REGION_UNICORN_TOWN, 0);

   784	        vm.expectEmit(true, false, false, true);
   785	        emit IClanWorldEvents.ImmediateMarketActionExecuted(

   790	            5e18,
   791	            ClanWorldConstants.RESOURCE_GOLD,

   798	        Clansman memory cs = world.getClansman(csId);

   800	        assertEq(uint8(r[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "immediate sell should be accepted");
   801	        assertEq(uint8(r[0].marketMode), uint8(MarketExecutionMode.Immediate), "sell should be immediate");
   802	        assertEq(afterClan.vaultWood, beforeClan.vaultWood, "vault wood should be unchanged");
   803	        assertEq(cs.carryWood, beforeCs.carryWood - 5e18, "carry wood should be sold immediately");
   804	        assertGt(afterClan.goldBalance, beforeClan.goldBalance, "gold should be credited immediately");
   805	        assertEq(uint8(cs.state), uint8(ClansmanState.WAITING), "worker should remain waiting");
   806	        assertEq(cs.currentRegion, ClanWorldConstants.REGION_UNICORN_TOWN, "worker should stay in town");
   807	        assertGt(cs.cooldownEndsAtTs, block.timestamp, "immediate action should consume cooldown");
   808	        assertFalse(world.getActiveMission(csId).active, "immediate action should not install a mission");

   891	    function test_immediateMarket_insufficientLiquidityFailsAndConsumesCooldown() public {
   892	        (, address woodAddr, uint32 clanId, uint32 csId) =
   893	            _setupHarnessClanAtWithWoodSeed(ClansmanState.WAITING, ClanWorldConstants.REGION_UNICORN_TOWN, 0, 5e18);
   894	
   895	        Clan memory beforeClan = world.getClan(clanId);
   896	
   897	        vm.expectEmit(true, false, false, true);
   898	        emit IClanWorldEvents.MarketActionFailed(
   899	            clanId,
   900	            csId,
   901	            ActionType.MarketBuy,
   902	            MarketExecutionMode.Immediate,
   903	            StatusCode.ERR_LIQUIDITY_INSUFFICIENT,

   180	
   181	### 4.3 Balance domains
   182	There are three balance domains:
   183	
   184	**Clansman carry domain**
   185	- wood
   186	- iron
   187	- wheat
   188	- fish
   189	
   190	Used for:
   191	- gathering
   192	- hauling
   193	- direct sell-to-market when physically in Unicorn Town
   194	- receiving bought resources from market actions
   195	
   196	**Clan vault domain**
   197	- wood
   198	- iron
   199	- wheat
   200	- fish
   201	
   202	Used for:
   203	- upkeep
   204	- winter burn
   205	- construction spending
   206	- bandit theft
   207	- OTC clan-to-clan resource transfers
   208	
   209	**Clan purse domain**
   210	- gold
   211	- blueprint fragments
   212	
   213	Used for:
   214	- market sale proceeds
   215	- market purchase spending
   216	- mercenary payments
   217	- OTC transfers of abstract assets
   218	
   219	### 4.4 Deposit rule
   220	Carried resources do not count for:
   221	- upkeep
   222	- winter burn
   223	- construction
   224	- bandit loot value
   225	- OTC transfers
   226	
   227	Carried resources only enter the clan vault after `DepositResources` resolves at homebase.
   228	
   229	### 4.5 Gold mining bonus rule
   230	If a worker hits the iron mining gold bonus:
   231	- the bonus gold is credited directly to the **clan gold purse** during settlement
   232	- no `carryGold` field exists
   233	
   234	Gold is never physically hauled by workers.
   235	
   560	`DefendBase` is a continuous mission that:
   561	- persists until interrupted by Elder
   562	- does not auto-complete after one bandit event
   563	- allows a worker to defend across multiple attacks if left in place
   564	
   565	### 8.3 MarketSell semantics
   566	`MarketSell` is **exact input only**.
   567	
   568	Meaning:
   569	- source = worker carry balance
   570	- destination = clan gold purse
   571	- `marketAmount` = exact amount of resource to sell
   572	- no `minOut` in v1
   573	
   574	### 8.4 MarketBuy semantics
   575	`MarketBuy` is **exact output only**.
   576	
   577	Meaning:
   578	- source = clan gold purse
   579	- destination = worker carry balance
   580	- `marketAmount` = exact amount of resource to buy
   581	- `maxGoldIn` = max purse gold willing to spend
   582	
   583	Validation:
   584	- requested output must fit within remaining carry capacity of that worker
   585	- if required gold at execution time exceeds `maxGoldIn`, buy fails
   586	- if clan purse lacks enough gold at execution time, buy fails
   587	
   588	### 8.5 Market token validation
   589	For v1:
   590	- `MarketSell.marketToken != goldToken`
   770	### 11.4 Reentrancy and interaction discipline
   771	Any function path that can trigger AMM interaction must follow checks-effects-interactions discipline.
   772	
   773	Recommended:
   774	- apply `nonReentrant` to immediate-order paths that can swap
   775	- apply `nonReentrant` to `heartbeat()` if it performs scheduled swaps
   776	- never leave partially updated clan/carry state around external calls
   777	
   778	Even if only known V2 pools are used in v1, implementation should still follow safe external-call patterns.
   779	
   780	---
   930	- a clansman may have at most one active mission
   931	- a dead clansman cannot receive orders
   932	- a dead clan cannot receive new workers or orders
   933	- only vault resources count for upkeep, winter burn, building, and bandit target value
   934	- bandits cannot spawn in Unicorn Town or Deep Sea
   935	- at most one active bandit troop exists at once
   936	- `Mission.startTick <= WorldState.currentTick`
   937	- `DefendBase` persistence is explicit and does not auto-complete
   938	- scheduled market actions are never lazily replayed; they resolve eagerly at heartbeat
   939	- OTC transfers cannot draw from worker carry balances
   940	- immediate market actions require `Waiting` + Unicorn Town + cooldown clear
   941	- sell is exact-input; buy is exact-output with `maxGoldIn`
   942	
   943	---
   944	
   945	## 16. Worked examples
   670	During camp:
   671	- they do not move
   672	- they do not attack
   673	- camp location is visible to all players
   674	
   675	## 6.7 Mutable targeting is intentional
   676	Bandit target selection is based on **current vault state at attack resolution time**, not at spawn time.
   677	
   678	This is an intentional design choice.
   679	
   680	### Intended consequences
   681	This intentionally allows agents to influence targeting during the warning window by:
   682	- moving resources out of vault into worker inventories
   683	- converting resources into gold where feasible
   684	- baiting or shifting target priority toward another base
   685	
  1095	## 12.4 Starting vault contents
  1096	A newly minted clan starts with:
  1097	- `20e18` wood
  1098	- `20e18` wheat
  1099	- `2e18` fish
  1100	- `0e18` iron
  1101	
  1102	These values are intended to prevent instant early starvation while still forcing quick economic action.
  1103	
  1104	## 12.5 Starting gold
  1105	A newly minted clan starts with:
  1106	- `3e18` gold
  1107	
  1108	This is intended to enable at least one meaningful early Unicorn Town trade.
  1109	
  1110	## 12.6 Starter mission state
  1111	Newly created clansmen start:
  1112	- physically at their homebase region

   181	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   182	    // Test 1: heartbeat rate limit
   183	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   184	
   185	    function test_heartbeat_rateLimit() public {
   186	        // First heartbeat should succeed (nextHeartbeatAtTs == block.timestamp)
   187	        world.heartbeat();
   188	        // Second heartbeat immediately after should revert
   189	        vm.expectRevert("ClanWorld: heartbeat rate limited");

   192	
   193	    function test_heartbeat_tickIncrementsExactlyOne() public {

   195	
   196	        world.heartbeat();

   198	        uint64 tickAfter = world.getWorldState().currentTick;

   201	
   202	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   203	    // Test 2: tick seed changes after heartbeat
   204	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   205	
   206	    function test_heartbeat_seedChanges() public {
   207	        WorldState memory beforeFirst = world.getWorldState();

   209	
   210	        bytes32 expectedFirstSeed =

   216	        assertTrue(afterFirst.currentTickSeed != beforeFirst.currentTickSeed, "seed should change after heartbeat");
   217	
   218	        vm.warp(block.timestamp + ClanWorldConstants.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_SECONDS);
   219	        bytes32 expectedSecondSeed =

   221	        world.heartbeat();
   222	
   223	        WorldState memory afterSecond = world.getWorldState();

   225	        assertTrue(afterSecond.currentTickSeed != afterFirst.currentTickSeed, "next seed should differ");

   227	            afterSecond.currentTickSeed != beforeFirst.currentTickSeed, "next seed should not repeat initial seed"

   719	
   720	    function _submitFirstClanMarketSells(uint32 clanId, address token, uint256 count) internal returns (uint256) {
   721	        ClanFullView memory view_ = world.getClanFullView(clanId);
   722	        require(count <= view_.clansmen.length, "too many clansmen");
   723	        ClanOrder[] memory orders = new ClanOrder[](count);
   724	        for (uint256 i = 0; i < count; i++) {
   725	            orders[i] = ClanOrder({
   726	                clansmanId: view_.clansmen[i].clansman.clansman.clansmanId,
   727	                gotoRegion: ClanWorldConstants.REGION_UNICORN_TOWN,
   728	                action: ActionType.MarketSell,
   729	                targetClanId: 0,
   730	                marketToken: token,

   732	                maxGoldIn: 0
   733	            });

   738	            assertEq(uint8(results[i].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "market sell should enqueue");
   739	        }
   740	        return count;

# Phase Super-Swarm Review (R2) — PR #198 (head fdcc04b)

## SUMMARY

Not clean. The carry rewrite fixes the core vault-vs-carry execution path for normal successful trades, and the cooldown bypass and storage-sort issues look addressed. But I found remaining correctness gaps around submit-time sell validation, the missing wheelbarrow vault-to-carry mechanic, CEI on buy swaps, and a still-vacuous empty-carry sell test.

## HIGH severity findings

1. **Market sells with insufficient carry are still accepted at submit time.**  
   `_validateAction` checks market token/amount and buy carry capacity, but never checks `MarketSell` carry balance. Scheduled sells can be accepted, consume cooldown/travel, then fail only at execution; immediate sells call `_executeImmediateMarket`, but `_processOrder` discards the returned failure and still returns `StatusCode.OK`.  
   See [ClanWorld.sol:1217](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1217), [ClanWorld.sol:1225](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1225), [ClanWorld.sol:2077](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2077), [ClanWorld.sol:2095](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2095), [ClanWorld.sol:1887](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1887).

2. **Wheelbarrow mechanic is not implemented in production code.**  
   The spec allows moving resources out of vault into worker inventories before bandit targeting, and the prompt explicitly calls out “travels to base, fills carry from vault.” This patch only implements carry-to-vault deposit. There is no reverse vault-to-carry path during order submission, travel settlement, or market preparation; tests mostly bypass this with harness `setCarry`. Starter vault resources therefore cannot be hauled to market or saved from a same-tick bandit raid.  
   See [ClanWorld.sol:1237](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1237), [ClanWorld.sol:1245](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1245), [ClanWorld.sol:326](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:326), [ClanWorld.sol:668](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:668), [ClanWorld.sol:1556](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1556).

## MEDIUM severity findings

1. **Market buys still perform the external pool swap before internal effects.**  
   For both immediate and scheduled buys, the pool swap happens before debiting clan gold and adding bought resources to carry. That leaves the buy path short of the requested CEI discipline, even though `nonReentrant` limits the current exploit surface.  
   See [ClanWorld.sol:1832](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1832), [ClanWorld.sol:1833](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1833), [ClanWorld.sol:1994](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1994), [ClanWorld.sol:2008](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2008).

2. **Scheduled market queue is no longer observable between submission and execution.**  
   The patch captures `commitSequence` at submit, but only pushes `ScheduledMarketAction` during mission settlement, immediately before heartbeat market execution. `getScheduledMarketActionsForTick` and `ScheduledMarketActionCommitted` no longer represent a pending queue for UI/indexers.  
   See [ClanWorld.sol:469](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:469), [ClanWorld.sol:1314](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1314), [ClanWorld.sol:1321](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1321), [ClanWorld.sol:891](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:891).

## LOW severity findings

1. **The empty-carry/full-vault sell test is still vacuous.**  
   `test_marketSell_fails_emptyCarry_fullVault` never calls `_setupMarket()`, so `woodToken` is zero and the order fails as unsupported token/market setup, not because carry is empty while vault is full.  
   See [ClanWorld.t.sol:2533](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:2533), [ClanWorld.t.sol:2547](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:2547).

2. **Dead mint/burn event surface remains in `IClanWorld`.**  
   External `mint`/`burn` are gone from `MinimalERC20`, but `ResourceMinted`/`ResourceBurned` were added to the main game ABI and are never emitted. This is small ABI/indexer noise.  
   See [IClanWorld.sol:590](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:590).

## Cross-cutting observations

Carry-based successful trades now use `cs.carry*` and leave vault balances untouched. Cooldown rejection before scheduled fallback looks fixed. The scheduled queue sort now sorts in memory and writes back once. External ERC20 mint/burn authority is removed, replaced with one-time `seedTreasury`.

Static review only; I did not run `forge test` in this read-only sandbox.

# Phase Super-Swarm Review (R2) — PR #198 (head fdcc04b)

## SUMMARY

Not clean. The carry rewrite fixes the core vault-vs-carry execution path for normal successful trades, and the cooldown bypass and storage-sort issues look addressed. But I found remaining correctness gaps around submit-time sell validation, the missing wheelbarrow vault-to-carry mechanic, CEI on buy swaps, and a still-vacuous empty-carry sell test.

## HIGH severity findings

1. **Market sells with insufficient carry are still accepted at submit time.**  
   `_validateAction` checks market token/amount and buy carry capacity, but never checks `MarketSell` carry balance. Scheduled sells can be accepted, consume cooldown/travel, then fail only at execution; immediate sells call `_executeImmediateMarket`, but `_processOrder` discards the returned failure and still returns `StatusCode.OK`.  
   See [ClanWorld.sol:1217](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1217), [ClanWorld.sol:1225](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1225), [ClanWorld.sol:2077](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2077), [ClanWorld.sol:2095](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2095), [ClanWorld.sol:1887](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1887).

2. **Wheelbarrow mechanic is not implemented in production code.**  
   The spec allows moving resources out of vault into worker inventories before bandit targeting, and the prompt explicitly calls out “travels to base, fills carry from vault.” This patch only implements carry-to-vault deposit. There is no reverse vault-to-carry path during order submission, travel settlement, or market preparation; tests mostly bypass this with harness `setCarry`. Starter vault resources therefore cannot be hauled to market or saved from a same-tick bandit raid.  
   See [ClanWorld.sol:1237](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1237), [ClanWorld.sol:1245](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1245), [ClanWorld.sol:326](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:326), [ClanWorld.sol:668](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:668), [ClanWorld.sol:1556](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1556).

## MEDIUM severity findings

1. **Market buys still perform the external pool swap before internal effects.**  
   For both immediate and scheduled buys, the pool swap happens before debiting clan gold and adding bought resources to carry. That leaves the buy path short of the requested CEI discipline, even though `nonReentrant` limits the current exploit surface.  
   See [ClanWorld.sol:1832](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1832), [ClanWorld.sol:1833](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1833), [ClanWorld.sol:1994](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1994), [ClanWorld.sol:2008](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2008).

2. **Scheduled market queue is no longer observable between submission and execution.**  
   The patch captures `commitSequence` at submit, but only pushes `ScheduledMarketAction` during mission settlement, immediately before heartbeat market execution. `getScheduledMarketActionsForTick` and `ScheduledMarketActionCommitted` no longer represent a pending queue for UI/indexers.  
   See [ClanWorld.sol:469](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:469), [ClanWorld.sol:1314](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1314), [ClanWorld.sol:1321](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1321), [ClanWorld.sol:891](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:891).

## LOW severity findings

1. **The empty-carry/full-vault sell test is still vacuous.**  
   `test_marketSell_fails_emptyCarry_fullVault` never calls `_setupMarket()`, so `woodToken` is zero and the order fails as unsupported token/market setup, not because carry is empty while vault is full.  
   See [ClanWorld.t.sol:2533](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:2533), [ClanWorld.t.sol:2547](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:2547).

2. **Dead mint/burn event surface remains in `IClanWorld`.**  
   External `mint`/`burn` are gone from `MinimalERC20`, but `ResourceMinted`/`ResourceBurned` were added to the main game ABI and are never emitted. This is small ABI/indexer noise.  
   See [IClanWorld.sol:590](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:590).

## Cross-cutting observations

Carry-based successful trades now use `cs.carry*` and leave vault balances untouched. Cooldown rejection before scheduled fallback looks fixed. The scheduled queue sort now sorts in memory and writes back once. External ERC20 mint/burn authority is removed, replaced with one-time `seedTreasury`.

Static review only; I did not run `forge test` in this read-only sandbox.
